title: 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards Winners Announced: Full List of Honorees and Award Recipients Revealed
body:
The music industry's elite gathered Sunday night for the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards, celebrating the best in music and honoring the artists who made it all happen. The ceremony, which aired live on television, saw a diverse range of winners across various categories, from pop and rock to country and hip-hop. Fans and artists alike eagerly awaited the announcement of the winners, and now that the dust has settled, it's clear that this year's awards were a true reflection of the industry's ever-changing landscape.

One of the most notable categories at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards was the Favorite Broadway Debut, which saw a talented group of newcomers vying for the top spot. In the end, Ashley Graham took home the award for her performance in the hit musical Chicago. Graham's win marked a significant milestone for the young actress, who has quickly become a rising star in the world of musical theater. Other notable winners in this category included Cheryl Porter for her role in & Juliet, Durrell Tank Babbs for Hells Kitchen, Gabrielle Nevaeh Green for Stranger Things: The First Shadow, and Jack Wolcott for his performance in a yet unidentified Broadway production.

The iHeartRadio Music Awards also recognized some of the biggest names in music today. The likes of Taylor Swift, Kendrick Lamar, and Billie Eilish all took home awards in various categories, solidifying their positions as some of the most influential artists in the industry. Meanwhile, newer artists like Olivia Rodrigo and The Kid LAROI made a strong showing, with Rodrigo taking home the award for Best Pop Album for her critically-acclaimed album Sour. The Kid LAROI, on the other hand, won Best New Artist, a nod to his rapid rise to fame in recent years.

In addition to the individual awards, the iHeartRadio Music Awards also honored some of the most iconic songs of the past year. The hit single "Shivers" by Ed Sheeran took home the award for Song of the Year, while the album of the year went to the critically-acclaimed release by Taylor Swift, Midnights. The awards also recognized some of the most influential artists of the past year, including Lizzo, who won the award for Best Female Artist, and Harry Styles, who took home the award for Best Male Artist.

The iHeartRadio Music Awards serve as a reminder of the ever-changing landscape of the music industry. With the rise of new artists and new sounds, the industry is constantly evolving, pushing the boundaries of what is considered "mainstream" music. As we look to the future, it's clear that the music industry will continue to be shaped by the diversity and creativity of its artists. The winners of the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards are a testament to this diversity and creativity, and they will undoubtedly continue to inspire and influence the music industry for years to come.

As the music industry continues to grow and evolve, it will be exciting to see how the winners of the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards will shape the industry in the years to come. With new talent emerging and new sounds being pushed to the forefront, there's never been a more exciting time to be a fan of music. The iHeartRadio Music Awards are a celebration of the music that brings us all together, and they serve as a reminder of the power of music to inspire and uplift us.
